identity.name: GRIT
identity.operator: single human, residential Las Vegas IP
identity.budget: zero paid infrastructure; GitHub Pages + GitHub Actions only

grit_bootstrap.mission: Harvest public monetizable event signals, normalize the entities behind them, score and cluster opportunities, and route them toward monetization systems — reducing the human labor required to operate multiple local revenue systems simultaneously. The end-state is a durable, location- independent income radar that finds activity earlier and organizes it better than competitors.

what_grit_is_not.item: a fabricator of data, activity, leads, or revenue

absolute_rules.rule: No synthetic data. No fake activity. No fabricated enrichment. Empty states are acceptable; simulated activity is forbidden.
Every system must connect to real monetization. Architecture without monetization is forbidden.
Human-in-the-loop execution is the design, not a stopgap. AI compresses labor; humans close transactions.
Free, durable hosting: static HTML, lightweight JS, local JSON, GitHub Pages + GitHub Actions. Degrade gracefully without AI.
Do not build for a phase whose predecessor has not produced real, validated flow.

Sanctioned channel first: open-data hub > bulk download > documented API > public-records request > polite low-volume residential capture. NEVER IP-evasion or proxy rotation. Output is acquisition/contractor signal, never a consumer report (bright FCRA line). Do not resell raw GIS (NRS 250) or recorded-document copies. Outreach rules (TCPA/DNC, NRS 645F) live at the routing layer, not at harvest. Staying clean IS the durability strategy -- a polite harvester does not get banned.

grit_bootstrap.strategic_shift: The 0.102 Assessor breakthrough solved data FRESHNESS. The bottleneck is now SIGNAL DENSITY. GRIT evolves from "parcel -> current owner" into a continuous city activity radar built on one chain: EVENT -> ENTITY -> MONEY. Something happens in the city, GRIT detects it, identifies who is involved, scores monetization probability, and routes it toward action. The full source landscape is catalogued in EVENT_MATRIX.md.

source.name: Clark County parcel candidates
source.details: Engine SAMPLES known owner/address layers, verifies real owner/address data is populated, and harvests the richest. Currently selects the NV statewide parcels (2018) layer because the modern county Assessor GIS layer exposes only APN + geometry. SSL cert verification disabled for gisgate.co.clark.nv.us due to a hostname-mismatched government certificate (documented).

source.name: Accela Citizen Access — permits (planned)
source.details: ViewState .aspx portal. Datacenter IPs are blocked (verified). Capture scaffold exists at grit/permits.py; calibrated parser comes after real captured samples. No events are published until a verified extractor exists (no fake data).

source.name: Clark County Assessor per-APN enrichment (LIVE in 0.102)
source.details: parceldetail.aspx?hdnparcel=APN&logo=1 returns the CURRENT assessor record via a plain GET. Deterministic label-anchored parser (grit/assessor.py) extracts owner, mailing, situs, current assessed + taxable value, last sale (price/date/type), land use, year built, beds, baths, roof, pool, lot size. Verified from cloud fetch; runs against the top CARDS_ENRICH_MAX leads each harvest, overwriting stale 2018 fields and emitting DEED timeline events. The freshness problem is solved.

pipeline.step: field auto-mapping with negative guards (prevents false matches)
entity normalization (PERSON / LLC / TRUST / HOA / GOVERNMENT / COMMERCIAL)
weighted opportunity scoring (entity + contactability + recency + value + cluster + events)
geographic cluster density (real radius calc, no fake density)
event join by parcel APN (timeline per card)

ui.feature: entity-colored markers with HOA/GOV hidden by default
trade-colored markers when trade data is present
Source Health Matrix (live + reachable + manual + pending + down)
per-card Assessor lookup link by APN

data_reality_findings.finding: Free clean GIS APIs cap at parcel geometry + APN (current) and owner/address (2018, ~25% addresses populated, 0% value).

Everything fresh and project-relevant (permits, deeds, current owner / value / sale) sits behind ViewState portals (Accela ACA, Assessor pcl.aspx, Recorder) that block datacenter IPs and which the county sells in bulk.
The free path to fresh data is therefore: clean APIs run on the GitHub Action; event-portal scraping runs LOCALLY from the operator's residential Las Vegas IP, low volume.

next_phase_priorities.priority: County permit ingestion (Accela B1): capture → calibrate parser → ingest PERMIT events (puller/value/date/APN).
Recorder distress ingestion (B3): DEED / Notice of Default / Trustee's Sale / liens by document-type + date.
SilverFlume entity ingestion (A3, bulk/API): LLC_REGISTRATION + officer/registered-agent graph.
NSCB license sync (A4): buyer list + permit-puller verification + license-status events.
Recency-weighted event scoring (events carry dates; weight by age).
Entity graph: repeat-entity detection + relationship clustering across permits/deeds/LLCs/licenses.
Spreadsheet operator console: multi-filter, disposition/contact state, saved searches.
City permits (B2), then validate C-tier (code/eviction/probate) one at a time with captured samples + compliance reads.

do_not.item: over-engineer agents before real acquisition flow exists

files_of_record.file: operating encyclopedia of monetizable sources (tiers, access, freshness, monetization, compliance)
version, entity tokens, candidate sources, weights
entity classification + weighted scoring + clustering + harvest

grit_bootstrap.judgement_test: Any proposed change must answer YES to at least one: - Does it find money sooner? - Does it organize money better? - Does it reduce labor per dollar? - Does it improve recency, monetizability, repeatability, automation, durability, or cost? If no — do not build it.
